metaTitle: Tip Calculator — Free Bill Splitting & Tip Calculator Online
metaDescription: Calculate tips and split bills instantly on your phone. Choose tip percentage, split between people, and see everyone's share. 100% free and works offline.

howto.description: Type in the total bill amount from your receipt.
Select a preset tip (10%, 15%, 18%, 20%, 25%) or enter a custom amount.
Set how many people are splitting the bill to see each person's share instantly.

why: Instant calculation — no button press needed
Split the bill between up to 20 people
Custom tip percentage for full flexibility
Shows tip amount, total, and per-person share clearly
Works completely offline — perfect for restaurants with no signal
No sign-up, no ads, completely free forever

faq.q: How much should I tip at a restaurant?
faq.a: In the US, the standard tip is 15-20% for sit-down restaurants. 18% is common for average service, 20% for good service, and 25% for exceptional service.

faq.q: How do I split a bill evenly?
faq.a: Enter the bill amount, choose your tip percentage, then set the number of people. The calculator will show exactly how much each person owes including tip.
Yes. Once the page is loaded, the calculator works completely offline. Perfect for restaurants with poor signal.

faq.q: Can I use a custom tip percentage?
faq.a: Yes. Tap the Custom button and enter any tip percentage you like.

faq.q: Is tax included in the tip calculation?
faq.a: The tip is calculated on the pre-tax bill amount by default, which is the standard practice. Enter your after-tax total if you prefer to tip on the full amount.

proTips: In the US, tipping 20% is considered standard for good service at sit-down restaurants.
For large groups, many restaurants automatically add an 18% gratuity — check your bill first!
When splitting unevenly, use the total amount and divide manually after seeing the per-person share.
troubleshooting: If the per-person amount looks off, double-check the number of people is set correctly.
For bills with tax already included, enter the full after-tax amount for the most accurate tip.
